rationale: Pure CSS-in-JS styling adjustments to two React components, `src/components/BudgetCommandCenter.jsx` and `src/components/IncomeHub.jsx`, to tighten the hero month-navigation block as the PR describes. Prev/Next buttons shrink (`marginTop: 18→12`, `height: 50→40`, `minWidth: 126→108`, `fontSize: 20→15`, `fontWeight: 900→700`, `letterSpacing: 0.4→0.35`). Month label drops from `fontSize: 28 / fontWeight: 900 / letterSpacing: 0.4` to `fontSize: 20 / fontWeight: 700 / letterSpacing: 0.2` with reduced `textShadow`. Heat-bar block tightens grid `gap: 14→10`, row gap `6→5`, header gap `12→10`, label font `11/900 → 10/800`, amount font `14/900 → 12/800`, bar height `14→11`, and `minWidth` of the fill from `12→10`. Both files receive the same set of numeric updates so the two tabs share inline-style values, satisfying the alignment goal. No logic, no tests, no cheat possible.
